extractedText:
--- PAGE 1 ---
Developer OS (Working Title)
Current public branding: Mudit Codes
Overview
Developer OS is a personal portfolio management and career progress tracking platform. It is initially
designed for personal use but is intended to evolve into a SaaS product for students, beginner developers,
and job seekers who want to manage their portfolios and track their professional growth from a single
platform.
Problem Statement
Many students and beginner developers neglect updating their portfolios because even small changes
often require manually editing source code and redeploying the website.
At the same time, learning progress is usually scattered across notebooks, to-do lists, coding platforms,
certificates, and personal notes, making it difficult to maintain an accurate representation of one's journey.
There is a need for a centralized system that allows users to manage their portfolio content and track their
career progress efficiently without directly modifying the codebase.
Proposed Solution
Developer OS aims to provide a centralized platform that combines portfolio management with career
progress tracking.
The platform will include a secure administration dashboard where portfolio content can be managed
through CRUD operations instead of editing source code.
Portfolio sections such as Hero, Projects, Skills, Achievements, Experience, Education, Certifications,
Resume, Contact Information, and Social Profiles should all be editable through the dashboard.
The system will also support multiple visual themes that can be switched without changing the application
code.
In addition, a skill tracking system will allow recording learning progress through multiple confidence levels
(Started, Basic, Intermediate, Advanced, Confident). Skills may optionally become visible on the public
portfolio automatically once they reach a desired level while still allowing manual control over visibility.
1



--- PAGE 2 ---
The overall objective is to keep the public portfolio synchronized with the developer's actual growth.
Initial Target Users
Phase 1
Myself (personal daily usage)
Future
Students
Beginner developers
Entry-level software engineers
Job seekers in technology
Why I Am Building This
I want a platform that documents my learning journey instead of being just another static portfolio website.
The project will allow me to:
Track my technical progress.
Record my career journey.
Update my portfolio without modifying source code.
Learn professional full-stack software development practices.
Continuously improve one long-term project as I gain new skills.
The long-term vision is to evolve this project into a mature product that can help other developers manage
their portfolios and professional growth in a structured way.
Future Vision (Not Part of V1)
Potential future features include:
AI career assistant
Personalized roadmap generation
AI-powered learning recommendations
Journaling system integrated with the portfolio
Automatic social media publishing
Multi-user support
